messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Hola!
messages.content_html: Hola!

messages.author: BobCat
messages.text: Hola, yo estoy en Cataluña pero el grupo es de gente de toda España. Varios miembros están en contacto con Portugal que están más avanzados y estudiando el ruido y ahora se sigue el estándar de Portugal, al reducir el ancho de banda a 62,5. Pasaros por el grupo de Telegram para ampliar visiones y conocimiento y debatir como unificar la península

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: I have checked Portugal and Spain. Most of them are using some random radio setup. There is no way to connect all this networks. Some are even using 433mhz... I would recommend to stick to EU narrow and eventually CR set to 5 (it will be compatible with CR8 but a little bit faster. This are the best settings in therms of noice level reduction/range/speed. I am setting all my nodes in Madrid zone to this standards
messages.content_html: I have checked Portugal and Spain. Most of them are using some random radio setup. There is no way to connect all this networks. Some are even using 433mhz... messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Hola! I confirm we are meshing EU/ narrow (optionally CR5 instead of standard CR8, as it is compatible and a bit faster) works verry well. It allows to lower Noise Floor that is crucial for the comunication especially in citi environment (a lot of electronic noise) And yes I confirm there are more and more people joining. We are having conversations with Colmenar, Tres Cantos, Getafe, Pozuelo,Alcobendas,Alcala and Madrid of course. Please join us on public when you come back frim Vigo ¡Saludos!

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Just select standard EU narrow preset from the list. You could change CR to 5 if you like. But standard 8 will work too

messages.author: DeKo xD
messages.text: Oh, I’m not from Madrid. I live in Porto . I’m starting the mesh there with some friends. Portugal’s mesh is a mess with all those 433 MHz nodes, but there’s almost no nodes in Porto.
messages.content_html: Oh, I’m not from Madrid. I live in Porto . I’m starting the mesh there with some friends. Portugal’s mesh is a mess with all those 433 MHz nodes, but there’s almost no nodes in Porto.

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Aa ok Good luck with building in Porto! There are a lot of dead reps/clients on the map A few weeks ago we started with 1 rep and now we are close to 20. Almost all of them are meshing and all are offgrid/solar, and we are building our own solar reps. If you see a rep with ZHP + a number it comes from same "factory" and was set by our team
messages.content_html: Aa ok Good luck with building in Porto! There are a lot of dead reps/clients on the map A few weeks ago we started with 1 rep and now we are close to 20. Almost all of them are meshing and all are offgrid/solar, and we are building our own solar reps. If you see a rep with ZHP + a number it comes from same "factory" and was set by our team

messages.author: DeKo xD
messages.text: Oh, about those dead nodes. You can ask for them to be deleted. Just check if they aren’t physically there before. I’ve done that a couple of times. What does ZHP stand for?
messages.content_html: Oh, about those dead nodes. You can ask for them to be deleted. Just check if they aren’t physically there before. I’ve done that a couple of times. What does ZHP stand for?

messages.author: rock5hock
messages.text: Enhorabuena! Cual es la razón por la que estais usando esta configuración en particular? Habeis hecho algunas pruebas para justificar la desviación del estándar europeo (eu narrow)? En Portugal decidieron hacerlo debido a su situación particular, pero no sé si en BCN habéis llegado a alguna prueba parecida? Creo que hay que unificar lo máximo posible con el resto de EU, y solo desviar en casos muy concretos y justificados, no te parece? (edited)
messages.content_html: Enhorabuena! Cual es la razón por la que estais usando esta configuración en particular? Habeis hecho algunas pruebas para justificar la desviación del estándar europeo (eu narrow)? En Portugal decidieron hacerlo debido a su situación particular, pero no sé si en BCN habéis llegado a alguna prueba parecida? Creo que hay que unificar lo máximo posible con el resto de EU, y solo desviar en casos muy concretos y justificados, no te parece? (edited)

messages.author: BobCat
messages.text: Se han realizado varias pruebas fuera de las interferencias de la banda de Meshtastic con la que se convive, así como con varios SF, y las frecuencias superiores de la banda de 868 van mejor, de momento el crecimiento con el preset de Portugal va mejor en urbano y en rural, con ancho de banda de 62,5 aunque pueda que el SF suba uno o dos puntos. El el grupo de Telegram están los estudios realizados, bandas de frecuencias, sf, y cr probados . Lo importante no es crecer en nodos a lo loco con Meshtastic en que se pierdan mensajes. La prioridad es no perder ni un mensaje entre repetidores
messages.content_html: Se han realizado varias pruebas fuera de las interferencias de la banda de Meshtastic con la que se convive, así como con varios SF, y las frecuencias superiores de la banda de 868 van mejor, de momento el crecimiento con el preset de Portugal va mejor en urbano y en rural, con ancho de banda de 62,5 aunque pueda que el SF suba uno o dos puntos. El el grupo de Telegram están los estudios realizados, bandas de frecuencias, sf, y cr probados . Lo importante no es crecer en nodos a lo loco con Meshtastic en que se pierdan mensajes. La prioridad es no perder ni un mensaje entre repetidores

messages.author: Qrius
messages.text: Hi. I hope it's ok that I write in English. Spain has a bunch of cr5 and sf8 areas, which is still compatible with the EU "standard" setting of cr8 and sf8. But I was curious why so much of Spain uses cr6 and sf7 which isn't compatible.
messages.content_html: Hi. I hope it's ok that I write in English. Spain has a bunch of cr5 and sf8 areas, which is still compatible with the EU "standard" setting of cr8 and sf8. But I was curious why so much of Spain uses cr6 and sf7 which isn't compatible.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Hi, Hard to say why. In Madrid zone are all set to EU narrow (plus CR5 in most cases, but it is compatible with CR8). And ther is well over 30 repeaters connected in our area. It works very well on EU narrow. The longest direct connection on this settings was 80km (client vs rpt) and ~50km between repeaters on NON directive antennas (3-5 dib) is a standard. So honestly I see no reason why to use Portugal legacy preset. Even in Potugal they are switching to EU narrow.
messages.content_html: Hi, Hard to say why. In Madrid zone are all set to EU narrow (plus CR5 in most cases, but it is compatible with CR8). And ther is well over 30 repeaters connected in our area. It works very well on EU narrow. The longest direct connection on this settings was 80km (client vs rpt) and ~50km between repeaters on NON directive antennas (3-5 dib) is a standard. So honestly I see no reason why to use Portugal legacy preset. Even in Potugal they are switching to EU narrow.

messages.author: Qrius
messages.text: It isn't only Portugal. It's most of the non-central areas of Spain. It's very strange.
messages.content_html: It isn't only Portugal. It's most of the non-central areas of Spain. It's very strange.

messages.author: smatias4
messages.text: for anything MC Portugal related, feel free to check https://www.meshcore.pt and telegram group https://t.me/MeshCorePT . We will be more than happy to answer all your doubts and questions. And BTW, 433 MHz is heavily used for MC in Portugal, together with the daily expanding 868 MHz and the mesh isn't a mess

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: This are not my words. Actually guy that lives in Lisbon, said that all was dead and they are building from the beginning. What is the reason to use this non standard radio settings?
messages.content_html: This are not my words. Actually guy that lives in Lisbon, said that all was dead and they are building from the beginning. What is the reason to use this non standard radio settings?

messages.author: smatias4
messages.text: There are no EU standards for presets, only community suggestions, that may or may not be voluntarily adopted in a particular region. What may apply to a set of countries does not constitute wide adoption, proof is that before the EU/UK narrow became public, other communities were already testing narrow presets, as the default 250k wide (collision with MC albeit with a different SF, plus all the other commercial LoRa services on 868) was not giving good results. We were already doing research for a 868 narrow preset in Portugal when the EU/UK narrow preset suggestion became public.
messages.content_html: There are no EU standards for presets, only community suggestions, that may or may not be voluntarily adopted in a particular region. What may apply to a set of countries does not constitute wide adoption, proof is that before the EU/UK narrow became public, other communities were already testing narrow presets, as the default 250k wide (collision with MC albeit with a different SF, plus all the other commercial LoRa services on 868) was not giving good results. We were already doing research for a 868 narrow preset in Portugal when the EU/UK narrow preset suggestion became public.

messages.author: smatias4
messages.text: Don't know who's the Lisbon guy, but all he had to do was to google "meshcore portugal" to find out that MC mesh was already a thing here.

messages.text: Sure, no problem at all. This is an open, community project. everyone chooses what suits them best. I personally prefere to have more universal setup, that works well, and gives a chance to connect wider area, international. even if it's not perfect
